currentFocus: Storage-conscious PatchBench routing-guard wave is integrated on main; the next developer slice is a compact PatchBench/BinaryPatchesPage presentation modularity hardening pass.

integratedWave.summary: Canonical integration adds shared reflected WinUI helper-output test support, PatchBench routing guards for BinaryPatchesPage online-readiness rendering, storage hygiene docs, and concise state reconciliation without relying on local campaign paths or unreachable worker commits as durable public truth.
integratedWave.routingGuard: BinaryPatchesPage online-readiness render paths are guarded to collect OnlineMultiplayerReadinessService state before calling PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText.Build and BuildCompanionSession, assign mapped text-state properties to controls, avoid inline literal text assignments in those render paths, and preserve exact-token order assertions that fail when either routing token is missing.
integratedWave.storagePolicy: LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md now records that campaign/build/package scratch is disposable after final reports and accepted findings are preserved, scratch devices are not durable sole storage, and Ghidra/proof pruning needs manifest classification.

storageCloseout.safeCleanup: Old local campaign scratch cleanup removed 10.926 GiB and did not delete or mutate tracked repo files, proof archives, Ghidra stores, copied game/runtime roots, installed game files, or original BEA.exe.
storageCloseout.ghidraBackups: Report-only/no-delete retention review kept about 142.295 GiB pending per-family manifest/provenance review; live project remains do-not-touch.
storageCloseout.proofArchives: Report-only/no-delete retention closeout kept about 270.438 GiB pending relative manifest/hash comparison; unknown H: containers remain do-not-touch.
storageCloseout.futureLocalMaintenance: Stale prunable Git worktree metadata remains a separate bounded local maintenance baton; start with dry-run evidence and do not delete branches or broad storage without fresh authority.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText remains presentation-only. BinaryPatchesPage still owns OnlineMultiplayerReadinessService calls and UI control assignment. AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.futureRuntimeRecommendation: Private Music Audible Output Live Bundle after repo/storage closeout and explicit runtime-proof leasing; original-binary online remains later and topology/material gated.
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs.

activeSlice.name: patchbench-binarypatchespage-presentation-modularity
activeSlice.goal: Extract one additional presentation-only PatchBench/BinaryPatchesPage text builder where it removes real duplication, then cover the boundary with focused UiTests while preserving current wording, routing, and non-claims.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: presentation/testability hardening only; no AppCore/service behavior move, Host/Join enablement, online action enablement, release, runtime, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, patch-row, byte-patch, launch, safe-copy manifest/signature, music, gameplay, visual, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference claim changes

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration validation uses git diff --check, JSON parse, stale local campaign path scan, docs/link/repo safety gates, WinUI build, focused PatchBench online-readiness UiTests, patch-engine safety, hard-payload safety, and WinUI primary-lane validation.
validation.nextSlice: Use focused PatchBench/WinUI tests plus relevant repo safety gates; rebuild WinUI before reflected helper-output tests.

nextSteps: Before the next write, verify live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main.
Run a compact PatchBench/BinaryPatchesPage presentation modularity slice with focused routing/helper-output tests.

Keep package scripts, release publication, Host/Join, online readiness, runtime audio, gameplay, visual, rebuild, no-noticeable-difference, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, and original-BEA mutation claims bounded to accepted evidence.
Treat stale Git worktree metadata cleanup and proof-archive manifest/hash comparison as separate local maintenance work requiring dry-run evidence and explicit scope.
